action_breakdown:
# Shot 37 websiteHero - Single-Shot Certification Asset

## Source timing

- Shot ID: shot_37
- Scene type: websiteHero
- Reference clip: C:\Users\Administrator\.codex\references\extracted-shots\shot_37_websiteHero_11.0-12.8.mp4
- Actual start time in source video: 10.958s
- Actual end time in source video: 12.792s
- Duration: 1.834s
- Certification status: candidate only; approved=false; allowedInFactory=false

## Visual hierarchy

1. Background layer: bright desk-like environment with very shallow parallax. Reusable version becomes a clean product-stage gradient and soft surface shadows, not the physical desk props.
2. Main website/laptop layer: centered laptop/browser surface, nearly front-facing. This is the dominant websiteHero object and differs from the already-certified angled push-in by being flatter and faster.
3. Browser content layer: generic hero media, headline bars, nav rail, and CTA placeholder. No source UI, brand, logo, or page text is reused.
4. Title/label layer: small product/website label or pill that clarifies context without covering the laptop.
5. CTA/value emphasis: one restrained spotlight or CTA chip near the lower hero area.
6. Camera hold: short readable settle, because the source shot is brief.

## Element timing, direction, speed, settle

- 0%-100% / heroDeskBackdropDrift: background drifts subtly left-to-right with very mild scale. Low speed, continuous.
- 4%-32% / laptopFrameRise: laptop frame rises from slight lower position with scale 0.94 to 1.0. Fast but non-bouncy; settles by 32%.
- 20%-48% / browserContentWipe: internal website sections wipe upward and fade in. Medium speed; should not feel like a slide deck.
- 34%-60% / titlePillReveal: context pill reveals from upper-left with small y-offset. Settles by 60%.
- 52%-78% / ctaSpotlightPulse: single CTA/value spotlight appears near the hero area with one controlled pulse.
- 78%-100% / heroHoldSettle: final composition holds with micro push-in for readability.

## Reusable atomic motions

- heroDeskBackdropDrift: soft website/product stage parallax.
- laptopFrameRise: centered laptop/browser rise and scale-in.
- browserContentWipe: generic website hero content reveal.
- titlePillReveal: small semantic label reveal.
- ctaSpotlightPulse: one-time CTA/value emphasis.
- heroHoldSettle: short final readability hold.

## Reference-only decoration to exclude

- Desk props, laptop brand silhouette details, exact source website content, source images, logo, and text labels are excluded.
- Avoid duplicating Shot 26's angled 3D push-in; this candidate should remain front-facing and compact.
- Avoid long camera orbit or repeated glow pulses because the shot is meant to be a quick bridge hero.

## Choreography timeline

| Track | Motion | Target | Percent | Role | Library decision |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | heroDeskBackdropDrift | heroDeskBackdrop | 0-100 | depth-establish | candidate |
| 2 | laptopFrameRise | laptopFrame | 4-32 | primary-website-entry | candidate |
| 3 | browserContentWipe | browserContent | 20-48 | website-content-reveal | candidate |
| 4 | titlePillReveal | titlePill | 34-60 | semantic-context | candidate |
| 5 | ctaSpotlightPulse | ctaSpotlight | 52-78 | conversion-emphasis | candidate |
| 6 | heroHoldSettle | fullComposition | 78-100 | readability-hold | candidate |

## QA notes

- Safe area: planned pass; centered laptop and labels remain inside margins.
- Typography: planned pass; generic placeholder text only.
- Semantic shape: planned pass; label, browser content, CTA, and centered hero have clear websiteHero roles.
- Current approval: false.
- Current factory access: false.
